-                its own family&rsquo;s output &mdash; maker&nbsp;&ne;&nbsp;judge at the model layer.
-              </p>
-              <p className="mono-line">

-                cross-family entailment, reverse prose&rarr;data, recall-favoring
-              </p>
-            </div>
-          </details>

-                </div>
+          <div className="lp-flow" role="list" aria-label="The five-stage checking flow">
+            {FLOW.map((s) => (
+              <div className={`lp-step${s.gate ? " gate" : ""}`} role="listitem" key={s.title}>
+                <Icon name={s.icon} className="lp-si" />
+                <span className="lp-sk">{s.kicker}</span>

-        <Reveal>
-          <details className="ds-tech">
-            <summary>
-              <Chevron /> For technical reviewers &middot; the real pipeline
-            </summary>
-            <div className="tech-body">
-              <p>
-                Stage 3 is a <em>deterministic</em> forward gate: declared claims are checked
-                claim&nbsp;&rarr;&nbsp;field and the output is byte-for-byte reproducible &mdash; the
-                fast, exact floor, pinned and eval-locked against a golden differential oracle. The
-                merchant&rsquo;s untrusted name never enters the model prompt (an injection cut), so
-                a hostile name can&rsquo;t steer the draft.
-              </p>
-              <p>
-                Stage 4 is a <em>cross-family entailment</em> judge that closes the gap from the
-                other direction (prose&nbsp;&rarr;&nbsp;data) &mdash; catching facts asserted in
-                prose but never declared as claims. Running it on a different model family keeps
-                maker&nbsp;&ne;&nbsp;judge at the model layer; it&rsquo;s tuned recall-favoring,
-                because a false hold costs a glance and a missed fabrication costs trust.
-              </p>
-            </div>
-          </details>
-        </Reveal>
       </section>

+      {/* ===== DIFFERENTIATION — a safety filter vs the facts ===== */}

 @import "tailwindcss";
 
 /* ============================================================================
-   ActivationOps — "Ledger Enterprise" design system (Stage 1 foundation)
-   Ported from mockups/v3-ledger-enterprise.html. Premium white ground, a single
-   vermilion accent (two lightnesses of one hue), cool-neutral ink/graphite, and
-   one signature primitive: the hairline-ruled ledger row (field · value · verdict).
-   Fonts are self-hosted via next/font/google (see app/layout.tsx) and reach this
-   file as CSS variables (--font-sans / --font-mono / --font-geist-mono).
+   Curbside Commons — "Oxblood" site design system (S1 foundation, 2026-07-08)
+   Spec: mockups/ultra-modern-2026-07-08/whole-site-gallery-v2.html (owner-picked).
+
+   RETHEME-IN-PLACE: every :root token NAME below is preserved (layout.tsx, Nav,
+   and the landing consume them) but the VALUES are remapped to Oxblood — a premium
+   off-white ground (#FBFBFD), a warm near-black ink ramp (harmonizing with the
+   accent, not cool-gray), wine-tinted hairlines, and ONE burgundy accent (#8A2233,
+   two lightnesses of one hue). An ADDITIVE "OXBLOOD SITE LAYER (S1)" section holds
+   the site-scoped tokens (wine ramp / severity / serif / ease) and the shared shell
+   primitives (nav + footer). Fonts still self-host via next/font/google (see
+   app/layout.tsx) as --font-sans / --font-mono / --font-geist-mono.
+
+   INTENTIONALLY UNTOUCHED (byte-for-byte, superseded by later slices):
+   • the entire `.rpt-wrap` report/demo Ledger block (line ~1105 → its print block)
+     — it redefines --paper/--ink/--body/--graphite/--hairline/--um/--sev-* locally,
+     so it is insulated from these :root remaps (S3 re-skins it to Oxblood);
+   • all `.ds-*` / `.cp-*` / `.lp-*` landing rule blocks — they inherit the new token
+     VALUES (new colors, old layout = the expected hybrid until S2 supersedes them).
    ========================================================================== */
 
 :root {
   color-scheme: light;
 
-  /* ground — true premium white, no warmth */
-  --bg: #ffffff;
-  --bg-2: #fcfcfd;
-  --bg-3: #f7f7f9;
-
-  /* ink + graphite structure (cool neutrals, AA+ on white) */
-  --ink: #0a0a0c;
-  --ink-2: #16161a;
-  --body: #34343c; /* 11.6:1 — body text */
-  --graphite: #52525c; /* 7.72:1 — labels, values */
-  --muted: #5d5d68; /* 6.50:1 — secondary mono */
-  --faint: #6c6c78; /* 5.16:1 — non-essential ≥13px */
-
-  /* hairlines */
-  --rule: #ececef;
-  --rule-2: #e0e0e4;
-  --rule-3: #d2d2d8;
-
-  /* THE single accent — vermilion, two lightnesses of one hue */
-  --signal: #e2402a; /* fills, dots, rules, marks */
-  --signal-text: #c0301b; /* 5.71:1 on white — small labels / verdicts */
-  --signal-tint: #fbedea; /* faint held-row ground */
-  --signal-rule: #f0d6cf; /* hairline at signal hue */
+  /* ground — premium off-white (NOT pure #fff; pure white+black is an AI-median
+     tell). Warm-neutral steps down. */
+  --bg: #fbfbfd;
+  --bg-2: #f8f7f8;
+  --bg-3: #f5f3f4;
+
+  /* ink + graphite structure — a WARM near-black ramp (R>G>B), harmonizing with
+     the wine accent. Contrast recomputed on the #fbfbfd ground; every step clears
+     the prior cool-gray token's documented ratio. */
+  --ink: #1a1712; /* 17.29:1 — headings, hard marks */
+  --ink-2: #2e2825; /* 14.05:1 — strong text */
+  --body: #423b38; /* 10.61:1 — body text (spec #463f3c was 9.98:1, darkened to clear ≥10:1) */
+  --graphite: #564d48; /* 7.97:1 — labels, values (≥ old 7.72) */
+  --muted: #615750; /* 6.80:1 — secondary mono (≥ old 6.50) */
+  --faint: #6d635b; /* 5.67:1 — non-essential ≥13px (≥ old 5.16) */
+
+  /* hairlines — wine-tinted (rgba of the wine-900 hue), darkening steps */
+  --rule: rgba(74, 14, 26, 0.1);
+  --rule-2: rgba(74, 14, 26, 0.17);
+  --rule-3: rgba(74, 14, 26, 0.26);
+
+  /* THE single accent — burgundy / oxblood, two lightnesses of one hue */
+  --signal: #8a2233; /* fills, dots, rules, marks */
+  --signal-text: #8a2233; /* 8.62:1 on #fbfbfd — small labels / verdicts (≥4.5) */
+  --signal-tint: rgba(138, 34, 51, 0.07); /* faint held-row ground */
+  --signal-rule: rgba(138, 34, 51, 0.24); /* hairline at signal hue */
 
   /* spacing — 4px base */
   --s1: 4px;
@@ -53,6 +68,33 @@
   --ff-sans: var(--font-sans), system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif;
   --ff-mono: var(--font-mono), var(--font-geist-mono), ui-monospace, monospace;
 
+  /* ==== OXBLOOD SITE LAYER (S1, 2026-07-08) — additive site-scoped tokens ==== */
+  /* wine ramp — the ONE brand hue, wine-900 (darkest) → wine-500 */
+  --wine-900: #3e0a13;
+  --wine-800: #55101d;
+  --wine-700: #6e1423;
+  --wine-600: #8a2233;
+  --wine-500: #9e3143;
+  /* accent aliases (the site layer reads these; = the wine ramp) */
+  --accent: #8a2233;
+  --accent-hi: #6e1423; /* hover / pressed */
+  --accent-soft: rgba(138, 34, 51, 0.07);
+  --accent-line: rgba(138, 34, 51, 0.24);
+  /* severity — kept perceptually FAR from the wine brand (always paired with a
+     shape + word by consuming surfaces, never colour alone) */
+  --error: #d92d20;
+  --error-soft: rgba(217, 45, 32, 0.08);
+  --warn: #b54708;
+  --warn-soft: rgba(181, 71, 8, 0.11);
+  --ok: #067647;
+  --ok-soft: rgba(6, 118, 71, 0.09);
+  /* serif display stack (brand wordmark + headlines) — no new dependency */
+  --serif: ui-serif, "New York", "Iowan Old Style", "Palatino Linotype", Palatino, Georgia,
+    "Times New Roman", serif;
+  /* shared easing */
+  --ease: cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1);
+  /* ==== end OXBLOOD SITE LAYER tokens ==== */
+
   font-synthesis: none;
   text-rendering: optimizeLegibility;
   -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased;
@@ -78,6 +120,121 @@ body {
   -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased;
 }
 
+/* ============================================================================
+   OXBLOOD SITE LAYER (S1, 2026-07-08) — shared shell primitives (Nav + footer).
+   Only what the global shell needs; landing/report/demo surfaces keep their own
+   blocks. Consumed by components/Nav.tsx and app/layout.tsx. Element selectors
+   `nav` / `footer` are hidden in the report @media print block (unchanged).
+   ========================================================================== */
+
+/* ----- Nav: sticky, hairline-ruled, serif wordmark, sans links (mockup spec) - */
+.site-nav {
+  position: sticky;
+  top: 0;
+  z-index: 40;
+  /* rgba(255,255,255,.88) IS the no-support fallback; blur is progressive */
+  background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.88);
+  backdrop-filter: saturate(1.4) blur(10px);
+  -webkit-backdrop-filter: saturate(1.4) blur(10px);
+  border-bottom: 1px solid var(--rule);
+}
+.site-nav-in {
+  max-width: var(--maxw);
+  margin: 0 auto;
+  display: flex;
+  flex-wrap: wrap;
+  align-items: center;
+  gap: 4px;
+  padding: 11px clamp(18px, 4vw, 36px);
+}
+.site-brand {
+  display: inline-flex;
+  align-items: center;
+  gap: 9px;
+  margin-right: 18px;
+  font-family: var(--serif);
+  font-weight: 600;
+  letter-spacing: -0.01em;
+  font-size: 16px;
+  color: var(--ink);
+  text-decoration: none;
+}
+.site-brand-mark {
+  width: 22px;
+  height: 22px;
+  flex: none;
+  color: var(--accent);
+}
+.site-navlink {
+  /* mockup spec: sans 13px (E-3 reviewer fix — the dispatch brief's "mono" was
+     the orchestrator's error; the mockup .navlink inherits the sans body font) */
+  font-family: var(--ff-sans);
+  font-size: 13px;
+  color: var(--muted);
+  text-decoration: none;
+  padding: 6px 11px;
+  border-radius: 7px;
+  white-space: nowrap;
+  transition:
+    background 0.16s var(--ease),
+    color 0.16s ease;
+}
+.site-navlink:hover {
+  background: var(--bg-3);
+  color: var(--ink);
+}
+.site-navlink[aria-current="page"] {
+  background: var(--accent);
+  color: #fff;
+}
+.site-navlink:focus-visible,
+.site-brand:focus-visible {
+  outline: 2px solid var(--accent);
+  outline-offset: 2px;
+}
+.site-status {
+  margin-left: auto;
+  display: inline-flex;
+  align-items: center;
+  gap: 7px;
+  font-family: var(--ff-mono);
+  font-size: 11px;
+  letter-spacing: 0.04em;
+  color: var(--muted);
+  background: var(--bg-3);
+  border: 1px solid var(--rule);
+  border-radius: 999px;
+  padding: 5px 11px;
+}
+.site-status-pip {
+  width: 6px;
+  height: 6px;
+  border-radius: 50%;
+  background: var(--accent);
+  flex: none;
+}
+
+/* ----- global footer: honesty paragraph on a hairline-ruled panel ----------- */
+.site-footer {
+  margin-top: 20px;
+  border-top: 1px solid var(--rule);
+  background: var(--bg-3);
+}
+.site-footer-in {
+  max-width: var(--maxw);
+  margin: 0 auto;
+  padding: 40px clamp(18px, 4vw, 36px) 72px;
+  font-size: 13px;
+  line-height: 1.62;
+  color: var(--body);
+}
+
+@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
+  .site-navlink {
+    transition: none;
+  }
+}
+
 /* ----- type primitives ------------------------------------------------------ */
 
 .ds-mono {
@@ -432,7 +589,7 @@ body {
   border-bottom: 1px solid var(--rule);
   display: flex;
   flex-wrap: wrap;
-  gap: 6px 18px;
+  gap: 8px;
   align-items: baseline;
 }
 .cp-rlbl {
@@ -441,13 +598,18 @@ body {
   color: var(--graphite);
   letter-spacing: 0.005em;
   width: 100%;
-  margin-bottom: 2px;
+  margin-bottom: 4px;
 }
+/* record fields as hairline chips (Oxblood v2 catch — matches the mockup .rf .f) */
 .cp-rf {
   font-family: var(--ff-mono);
   font-size: 11.5px;
   color: var(--ink);
   white-space: nowrap;
+  background: var(--bg);
+  border: 1px solid var(--rule);
+  border-radius: 6px;
+  padding: 3px 8px;
 }
 .cp-rf .cp-rk {
   color: var(--graphite);
@@ -633,458 +795,340 @@ body {
 }

 }

-  display: none;

+  display: block;
+  font-family: var(--ff-mono);
+  font-size: 10px;
+  letter-spacing: 0.07em;

+  margin-bottom: 6px;
 }

-  width: 13px;
-  height: 13px;
-  flex: none;

   color: var(--signal-text);
-  transition: transform 0.22s cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1);
 }
-.ds-tech[open] > summary .chev {
-  transform: rotate(90deg);
+.lp-step .lp-h4 {
+  margin-bottom: 6px;
 }
-.ds-tech .tech-body {
-  padding: 2px 2px 22px 24px;
+@media (max-width: 760px) {
+  .lp-flow {
+    grid-template-columns: 1fr;
+  }
+  .lp-step {
+    border-left: none;
+    border-top: 1px solid var(--rule);
+  }
+  .lp-step:first-child {
+    border-top: none;
+  }
 }
-.ds-tech .tech-body p {
-  font-size: 15px;
-  line-height: 1.62;
+
+/* ----- CLOSE — honesty note + mono strip ------------------------------------ */
+.lp-note {
+  margin: 26px 0 0;
+  border: 1px solid var(--rule);
+  background: var(--bg-3);
+  border-radius: 10px;
+  padding: 14px 16px;
+  font-size: 13px;
+  line-height: 1.6;
   color: var(--body);
-  max-width: 72ch;
-  margin: 0;
-}

-  margin-top: 12px;
-}
-.ds-tech .tech-body em {
-  font-style: normal;
-  color: var(--ink);
-  font-weight: 600;
-}
-.ds-tech .tech-body .mono-line {
-  font-family: var(--ff-mono);
-  font-size: 12px;

+  max-width: 82ch;
 }
-.ds-tech .tech-body .mono-line .k {
-  color: var(--graphite);
+.lp-strip {
+  margin: 16px 0 0;
+  padding-top: 14px;
+  border-top: 1px solid var(--rule);
+  font-size: 11px;
+  line-height: 1.8;
+  letter-spacing: 0.005em;
+  color: var(--faint);
+  max-width: 82ch;
 }
 
 @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
-  .ds-tech > summary .chev {
+  .lp-btn,
+  .lp-btn .lp-ic,
+  .lp-panel,
+  .lp-step {
     transition: none;
   }
 }

codex
The diff is large but localized: `app/page.tsx` and `app/globals.css` only for this slice, with no component file diff under `components/landing`. One thing I’m pressure-testing now is whether the redesign removed any old class that still appears in JSX, because that is the easiest way this kind of landing rewrite regresses silently.

 succeeded in 0ms:
import type { Metadata } from "next";
import type { ReactNode } from "react";
import { Geist, Geist_Mono, JetBrains_Mono } from "next/font/google";
import { Nav } from "@/components/Nav";
import "./globals.css";

// Self-hosted via next/font/google (no render-blocking <link>). Geist = UI/display,
// JetBrains Mono = the tabular ledger numerals / field keys / verdict labels, Geist Mono
// kept available as a second mono. Exposed as CSS variables consumed in globals.css.
const geist = Geist({
  subsets: ["latin"],
  variable: "--font-sans",
  display: "swap",
});
const geistMono = Geist_Mono({
  subsets: ["latin"],
  variable: "--font-geist-mono",
  display: "swap",
});
const jetbrainsMono = JetBrains_Mono({
  subsets: ["latin"],
  variable: "--font-mono",
  display: "swap",
});

export const metadata: Metadata = {
  title: {
    default: "Curbside Commons — deterministic commerce-truth verifier (simulated prototype)",
    template: "%s · Curbside Commons",
  },
  description:
    "The truth layer for agentic commerce — a deterministic verifier of platform/AI-agent serving copies vs the merchant system-of-record, UCP conformance, and NYC fee-cap audit. Simulated corpus; company-agnostic prototype.",
};

export default function RootLayout({ children }: { children: ReactNode }) {
  return (
    <html
      lang="en"
      data-scroll-behavior="smooth"
      className={`${geist.variable} ${geistMono.variable} ${jetbrainsMono.variable}`}
    >
      <body>
        <a href="#main-content" className="ds-skip">
          Skip to main content
        </a>
        <Nav />
        <div id="main-content" tabIndex={-1}>
          {children}
        </div>
        <footer className="site-footer">
          <div className="site-footer-in">
            <span style={{ fontWeight: 600, color: "var(--ink)" }}>
              Demo / portfolio prototype.
            </span>{" "}
            REPLAY over <span style={{ fontWeight: 600 }}>fictional display names</span> +{" "}
            <span style={{ fontWeight: 600 }}>synthetic activation state</span> (the adapter
            ingests real public DataSF records; the demo shows invented ones) — not production
            logs, real sends, real marketplace access, or real-impact data. The &ldquo;real
            Gemini&rdquo; output shown is a{" "}
            <span style={{ fontWeight: 600 }}>recorded static fixture</span> (reproduce it
            locally with your own key). Human-led, AI-assisted, professionally reviewed.{" "}
            <span style={{ fontWeight: 600 }}>
              Not affiliated with, endorsed by, or connected to
            </span>{" "}
            DoorDash, Uber Eats, Grubhub, DataSF, or any named business.
          </div>
        </footer>
      </body>
    </html>
  );
}

     1	"use client";
     2	
     3	import { useEffect, useRef } from "react";
     4	
     5	/**
     6	 * The SHOWN catch — the differentiator centerpiece.
     7	 *
     8	 * The result is KNOWN and recorded, so every verdict + the "Held" banner are present in the
     9	 * server-rendered HTML in their settled (resolved) state. JavaScript only *replays* a staggered
    10	 * reveal as the panel scrolls into view, and ONLY when reduced-motion is off. Under reduced
    11	 * motion (the e2e runs reduced-motion) the panel renders fully settled with no animation — the
    12	 * verdicts, the hold, and the reviewer bar are all visible in the initial DOM.
    13	 *
    14	 * Two ledger lines match the merchant's own record; one confident-sounding claim — a go-live
    15	 * date that simply isn't on the record — is caught and held for a person. Plain verdicts only;
    16	 * the deep mechanics live one <details> reveal down (Layer 2), never in this always-visible DOM.
    17	 */
    18	
    19	type Result = "pass" | "fail";
    20	
    21	const CLAIMS: Array<{ text: string; result: Result; cite: string }> = [
    22	  {
    23	    text: "“You’ve completed 2 of 5 setup steps.”",
    24	    result: "pass",
    25	    cite: "→ setup: 2 of 5 steps",
    26	  },
    27	  {
    28	    text: "“Add photos to finish your listing.”",
    29	    result: "pass",
    30	    cite: "→ blocker: photos needed",
    31	  },
    32	  {
    33	    text: "“You’ll be live by Friday.”",
    34	    result: "fail",
    35	    cite: "no go-live date on this record",
    36	  },
    37	];
    38	
    39	function CheckGlyph() {
    40	  return (
    41	    <svg className="cp-glyph" viewBox="0 0 15 15" fill="none" aria-hidden="true">
    42	      <path
    43	        d="M3 8l3 3 6-7"
    44	        stroke="var(--ink)"
    45	        strokeWidth="1.9"
    46	        strokeLinecap="round"
    47	        strokeLinejoin="round"
    48	      />
    49	    </svg>
    50	  );
    51	}
    52	
    53	function CrossGlyph() {
    54	  return (
    55	    <svg className="cp-glyph" viewBox="0 0 15 15" fill="none" aria-hidden="true">
    56	      <path d="M4 4l7 7M11 4l-7 7" stroke="var(--signal-text)" strokeWidth="1.9" strokeLinecap="round" />
    57	    </svg>
    58	  );
    59	}
    60	
    61	export function CatchPanel() {
    62	  const ref = useRef<HTMLDivElement | null>(null);
    63	
    64	  // SSR renders the settled state (`cp-settled`). Class toggles happen imperatively here — never
    65	  // via synchronous setState in the effect body — so there are no cascading renders. Under reduced
    66	  // motion we leave the settled DOM untouched.
    67	  useEffect(() => {
    68	    const el = ref.current;
    69	    if (!el || typeof window === "undefined") return;
    70	    if (window.matchMedia("(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)").matches) return;
    71	
    72	    // motion allowed: drop to the un-resolved state, then replay on scroll-in
    73	    el.classList.remove("cp-settled");
    74	    const io = new IntersectionObserver(
    75	      (entries) => {
    76	        for (const e of entries) {
    77	          if (e.isIntersecting) {
    78	            e.target.classList.add("cp-on");
    79	            io.disconnect();
    80	          }
    81	        }
    82	      },
    83	      { threshold: 0.4 },
    84	    );
    85	    io.observe(el);
    86	    return () => io.disconnect();
    87	  }, []);
    88	
    89	  return (
    90	    <div
    91	      ref={ref}
    92	      className="ds-panel ds-panel-strong cp cp-settled"
    93	      aria-label="A recorded draft, checked line by line against this merchant's record"
    94	    >
    95	      <div className="cp-cap">
    96	        <span>outreach draft &middot; checked against the record</span>
    97	        <span className="cp-cap-right">
    98	          <span className="cp-tick" aria-hidden="true" />
    99	          held for review
   100	        </span>
   101	      </div>
   102	
   103	      {/* the merchant's own record — the only ground truth */}
   104	      <div className="cp-record" aria-label="This merchant's record">
   105	        <span className="cp-rlbl">
   106	          this merchant&rsquo;s record &middot; the only thing the message is checked against
   107	        </span>
   108	        <span className="cp-rf">
   109	          <span className="cp-rk">category:</span> <span className="cp-rv">Restaurant</span>
   110	        </span>
   111	        <span className="cp-rf">
   112	          <span className="cp-rk">setup:</span>{" "}
   113	          <span className="cp-rv ds-num">2 of 5 steps</span>
   114	        </span>
   115	        <span className="cp-rf">
   116	          <span className="cp-rk">blocker:</span> <span className="cp-rv">photos needed</span>
   117	        </span>
   118	        <span className="cp-rf absent">
   119	          <span className="cp-rk">go-live date:</span> <span className="cp-rv">none on file</span>
   120	        </span>
   121	      </div>
   122	
   123	      <div className="cp-draft">
   124	        {CLAIMS.map((c, i) => (
   125	          <div key={i} className={`cp-claim ${c.result}`}>
   126	            <div className="cp-ctext">{c.text}</div>
   127	            <div className="cp-verdict">
   128	              <span className={`cp-vcell ${c.result}`}>
   129	                {c.result === "pass" ? <CheckGlyph /> : <CrossGlyph />}
   130	                {c.result === "pass" ? "matches the data" : "not in the data"}
   131	              </span>
   132	              <span className="ds-mono cp-cite">{c.cite}</span>
   133	            </div>
   134	          </div>
   135	        ))}
   136	
   137	        <div className="cp-held">
   138	          <span className="cp-held-lbl">Held for a person to approve.</span>
   139	          <span className="ds-mono">
   140	            1 claim the data doesn&rsquo;t back &middot; not sent, not rejected &mdash; it&rsquo;s
   141	            your call.
   142	          </span>
   143	        </div>
   144	
   145	        <div className="cp-decide" aria-label="Reviewer actions">
   146	          <span className="cp-dnote ds-mono">
   147	            what you do next &middot; recorded to the audit trail
   148	          </span>
   149	          <button className="ds-btn hold" type="button" disabled aria-disabled="true">
   150	            Keep held
   151	          </button>
   152	          <button className="ds-btn" type="button" disabled aria-disabled="true">
   153	            Edit the claim
   154	          </button>
   155	          <button className="ds-btn primary" type="button" disabled aria-disabled="true">
   156	            Approve &amp; send
   157	          </button>
   158	        </div>
   159	      </div>
   160	    </div>
   161	  );
   162	}

   492	:focus-visible {

   514	/* Scroll-reveal helpers — resolved to settled state under reduced motion. */
   515	.ds-reveal {
   516	  opacity: 0;

   518	  transition:

   556	/* ----- the CatchPanel centerpiece (cp-*) ------------------------------------ */
   557	/* SSR renders settled (.cp-settled or .cp-on). Animation only via .cp class
   558	   without .cp-on (un-resolved), resolved by .cp-on or .cp-settled. */
   559	.cp-cap {
   560	  display: flex;
   561	  align-items: center;
   562	  justify-content: space-between;
   563	  gap: 12px;
   564	  padding: 13px 16px;
   565	  border-bottom: 1px solid var(--rule-2);
   566	  font-family: var(--ff-mono);
   567	  font-size: 11.5px;
   568	  letter-spacing: 0.005em;
   569	  color: var(--graphite);
   570	  background: var(--bg-2);
   571	}
   572	.cp-cap-right {
   573	  color: var(--signal-text);
   574	  display: inline-flex;
   575	  align-items: center;
   576	  gap: 7px;
   577	}
   578	.cp-tick {
   579	  width: 6px;
   580	  height: 6px;
   581	  border-radius: 999px;
   582	  background: var(--signal);
   583	  flex: none;
   584	}
   585	
   586	.cp-record {
   587	  padding: 13px 16px;
   588	  background: var(--bg-2);
   589	  border-bottom: 1px solid var(--rule);
   590	  display: flex;
   591	  flex-wrap: wrap;
   592	  gap: 8px;
   593	  align-items: baseline;
   594	}
   595	.cp-rlbl {
   596	  font-family: var(--ff-mono);
   597	  font-size: 11px;
   598	  color: var(--graphite);
   599	  letter-spacing: 0.005em;
   600	  width: 100%;
   601	  margin-bottom: 4px;
   602	}
   603	/* record fields as hairline chips (Oxblood v2 catch — matches the mockup .rf .f) */
   604	.cp-rf {
   605	  font-family: var(--ff-mono);
   606	  font-size: 11.5px;
   607	  color: var(--ink);
   608	  white-space: nowrap;
   609	  background: var(--bg);
   610	  border: 1px solid var(--rule);
   611	  border-radius: 6px;
   612	  padding: 3px 8px;
   613	}
   614	.cp-rf .cp-rk {
   615	  color: var(--graphite);
   616	}
   617	.cp-rf.absent .cp-rv {
   618	  color: var(--signal-text);
   619	}
   620	.cp-rf .cp-rv {
   621	  font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums;
   622	}
   623	
   624	.cp-draft {
   625	  padding: 0;
   626	}
   627	.cp-claim {
   628	  padding: 16px 16px;
   629	  border-top: 1px solid var(--rule);
   630	  opacity: 1;
   631	  transform: none;
   632	  transition:
   633	    opacity 0.5s ease,
   634	    transform 0.5s ease,
   635	    background 0.45s ease;
   636	}
   637	.cp-claim:first-child {
   638	  border-top: none;
   639	}
   640	.cp-claim.fail {
   641	  background: var(--signal-tint);
   642	}
   643	.cp-ctext {
   644	  font-size: 15.5px;
   645	  line-height: 1.45;
   646	  color: var(--ink);
   647	  letter-spacing: -0.01em;
   648	}
   649	.cp-verdict {
   650	  margin-top: 11px;
   651	  display: flex;
   652	  align-items: center;
   653	  gap: 12px;
   654	  flex-wrap: wrap;
   655	  font-family: var(--ff-mono);
   656	  font-size: 11.5px;
   657	  letter-spacing: 0.005em;
   658	  opacity: 1;
   659	  transition: opacity 0.35s ease;
   660	}
   661	.cp-vcell {
   662	  display: inline-flex;
   663	  align-items: center;
   664	  gap: 8px;
   665	  font-weight: 500;
   666	  padding: 3px 0;
   667	}
   668	.cp-vcell.pass {
   669	  color: var(--ink);
   670	}
   671	.cp-vcell.fail {
   672	  color: var(--signal-text);
   673	}
   674	.cp-glyph {
   675	  width: 15px;
   676	  height: 15px;
   677	  flex: none;
   678	  transform: scale(1);
   679	  opacity: 1;
   680	  transition:
   681	    transform 0.42s cubic-bezier(0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1),
   682	    opacity 0.3s ease;
   683	}
   684	.cp-cite {
   685	  color: var(--muted);
   686	}
   687	
   688	.cp-held {
   689	  padding: 16px 16px;
   690	  border-top: 1px solid var(--signal-rule);
   691	  background: var(--signal-tint);
   692	  display: flex;
   693	  align-items: baseline;
   694	  gap: 10px;
   695	  flex-wrap: wrap;
   696	  opacity: 1;
   697	  transform: none;
   698	  transition:
   699	    opacity 0.5s ease,
   700	    transform 0.5s ease;
   701	}
   702	.cp-held-lbl {
   703	  font-size: 14px;
   704	  font-weight: 600;
   705	  color: var(--signal-text);
   706	  letter-spacing: -0.01em;
   707	}
   708	.cp-held .ds-mono {
   709	  font-size: 11.5px;
   710	  color: var(--signal-text);
   711	}
   712	
   713	.cp-decide {
   714	  padding: 14px 16px;
   715	  border-top: 1px solid var(--rule-2);
   716	  background: var(--bg-2);
   717	  display: flex;
   718	  align-items: center;
   719	  gap: 10px;
   720	  flex-wrap: wrap;
   721	  opacity: 1;
   722	  transition: opacity 0.5s ease;
   723	}
   724	.cp-dnote {
   725	  font-size: 11px;
   726	  color: var(--muted);
   727	  margin-right: auto;
   728	}
   729	
   730	/* Animated (motion-on) state: start un-resolved, resolve in a stagger when .cp-on lands.
   731	   .cp-settled is the SSR/reduced-motion path and never enters this branch. */
   732	.cp:not(.cp-settled):not(.cp-on) .cp-claim {
   733	  opacity: 0;
   734	  transform: translateY(7px);
   735	}
   736	.cp:not(.cp-settled):not(.cp-on) .cp-claim .cp-verdict {
   737	  opacity: 0;
   738	}
   739	.cp:not(.cp-settled):not(.cp-on) .cp-glyph {
   740	  transform: scale(0.4);
   741	  opacity: 0;
   742	}
   743	.cp:not(.cp-settled):not(.cp-on) .cp-held,
   744	.cp:not(.cp-settled):not(.cp-on) .cp-decide {
   745	  opacity: 0;
   746	}
   747	.cp:not(.cp-settled):not(.cp-on) .cp-held {
   748	  transform: translateY(6px);
   749	}
   750	
   751	.cp.cp-on .cp-claim:nth-of-type(1) {
   752	  transition-delay: 0.05s;
   753	}
   754	.cp.cp-on .cp-claim:nth-of-type(2) {
   755	  transition-delay: 0.55s;
   756	}
   757	.cp.cp-on .cp-claim:nth-of-type(3) {
   758	  transition-delay: 1.1s;
   759	}
   760	.cp.cp-on .cp-claim:nth-of-type(1) .cp-verdict {
   761	  transition-delay: 0.48s;
   762	}
   763	.cp.cp-on .cp-claim:nth-of-type(2) .cp-verdict {
   764	  transition-delay: 1s;
   765	}
   766	.cp.cp-on .cp-claim:nth-of-type(3) .cp-verdict {
   767	  transition-delay: 1.7s;
   768	}
   769	.cp.cp-on .cp-held {
   770	  transition-delay: 2.2s;
   771	}
   772	.cp.cp-on .cp-decide {
   773	  transition-delay: 2.5s;
   774	}
   775	
   776	@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
   777	  html {
   778	    scroll-behavior: auto;
   779	  }
   780	  .ds-reveal,
   781	  .ds-stagger > * {
   782	    opacity: 1 !important;
   783	    transform: none !important;
   784	    transition: none !important;
   785	  }
   786	  .cp .cp-claim,
   787	  .cp .cp-verdict,
   788	  .cp .cp-held,
   789	  .cp .cp-decide,
   790	  .cp .cp-glyph {
   791	    opacity: 1 !important;
   792	    transform: none !important;
   793	    transition: none !important;
   794	  }
   795	}
   796	
   797	/* ============================================================================
   798	   OXBLOOD LANDING (S2, 2026-07-08) — the editorial scroll-story on the S1 tokens.
   799	   Spec: the #/ Landing view of whole-site-gallery-v2.html (owner-picked v2). Serif
   800	   display headlines (scoped to .lp-main), ONE wine accent, hairline-ruled sections,
   801	   the five-stage flow as a hairline-CONNECTED strip (gate stages emphasised, never
   802	   numbered 01/02/03). No gradients, no glass, no icon-in-square tiles, radii <= 12px.
   803	   Replaces the interim lp-* / ds-tech landing block (both were app/page.tsx-only).
   804	   The CatchPanel keeps its own cp-* block above (SSR-settled + reduced-motion contract).
   805	   ========================================================================== */
   806	
   807	/* serif display headlines — scoped to the landing (matches the v2 spec register) */
   808	.lp-main h1,
   809	.lp-main h2,
   810	.lp-main h3,
   811	.lp-main h4 {
   812	  font-family: var(--serif);
   813	  font-weight: 600;
   814	  letter-spacing: -0.012em;
   815	  color: var(--ink);
   816	  margin: 0;
   817	  text-wrap: balance;
   818	}
   819	
   820	/* ----- shared landing atoms ------------------------------------------------- */
   821	.lp-ic {
   822	  width: 1.05em;
   823	  height: 1.05em;
   824	  flex: none;
   825	  vertical-align: -2px;
   826	}
   827	.lp-si {
   828	  display: block;
   829	  width: 26px;
   830	  height: 26px;
   831	  color: var(--accent);
   832	  margin-bottom: 12px;
   833	}
   834	.lp-eyebrow {
   835	  display: inline-flex;
   836	  align-items: center;
   837	  gap: 8px;
   838	  margin: 0 0 16px;
   839	  font-family: var(--ff-mono);
   840	  font-size: 11.5px;
   841	  font-weight: 600;
   842	  letter-spacing: 0.14em;
   843	  text-transform: uppercase;
   844	  color: var(--accent);
   845	}
   846	.lp-h2 {
   847	  font-size: clamp(25px, 3.8vw, 40px);
   848	  line-height: 1.08;
   849	  max-width: 20ch;
   850	}

 succeeded in 0ms:
   850	}
   851	.lp-h3 {
   852	  font-size: 18px;
   853	  line-height: 1.25;
   854	}
   855	.lp-h4 {
   856	  font-size: 15px;
   857	  line-height: 1.3;
   858	}
   859	.lp-lede {
   860	  margin: 16px 0 0;
   861	  font-size: clamp(15.5px, 1.7vw, 17px);
   862	  line-height: 1.55;
   863	  color: var(--body);
   864	  max-width: 56ch;
   865	  text-wrap: pretty;
   866	}
   867	.lp-lede-wide {
   868	  max-width: 64ch;
   869	}
   870	.lp-lede em,
   871	.lp-foot em,
   872	.lp-ptext em {
   873	  font-style: normal;
   874	  font-weight: 600;
   875	  color: var(--ink);
   876	}
   877	.lp-ptext {
   878	  margin: 8px 0 0;
   879	  font-size: 14px;
   880	  line-height: 1.5;
   881	  color: var(--body);
   882	}
   883	.lp-foot {
   884	  margin: 20px 0 0;
   885	  font-size: 15px;
   886	  line-height: 1.55;
   887	  color: var(--body);
   888	  max-width: 70ch;
   889	}
   890	.lp-mark {
   891	  color: var(--signal-text);
   892	  white-space: nowrap;
   893	}
   894	
   895	/* ----- buttons (wine primary — the ONE accent doing the primary action) ----- */
   896	.lp-cta-row {
   897	  display: flex;
   898	  flex-wrap: wrap;
   899	  align-items: center;
   900	  gap: 14px;
   901	  margin-top: 28px;
   902	}
   903	.lp-cta-close {
   904	  margin-top: 24px;
   905	}
   906	.lp-cta-note {
   907	  font-family: var(--ff-mono);
   908	  font-size: 12px;
   909	  color: var(--muted);
   910	}
   911	.lp-btn {
   912	  display: inline-flex;
   913	  align-items: center;
   914	  gap: 8px;
   915	  font-family: var(--ff-sans);
   916	  font-size: 14px;
   917	  font-weight: 600;
   918	  letter-spacing: -0.006em;
   919	  text-decoration: none;
   920	  padding: 11px 18px;
   921	  border-radius: 8px;
   922	  border: 1px solid transparent;
   923	  min-height: 44px;
   924	  cursor: pointer;
   925	  transition:
   926	    transform 0.16s var(--ease),
   927	    background 0.16s ease,
   928	    border-color 0.16s ease,
   929	    box-shadow 0.16s ease;
   930	}
   931	.lp-btn.primary {
   932	  background: var(--accent);
   933	  color: #fff;
   934	  box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(74, 14, 26, 0.12);
   935	}
   936	.lp-btn.primary:hover {
   937	  background: var(--accent-hi);
   938	  transform: translateY(-1px);
   939	  box-shadow: 0 6px 22px rgba(74, 14, 26, 0.16);
   940	}
   941	.lp-btn.ghost {
   942	  background: transparent;
   943	  border-color: var(--rule-2);
   944	  color: var(--ink);
   945	}
   946	.lp-btn.ghost:hover {
   947	  background: var(--bg-3);
   948	  border-color: var(--accent-line);
   949	}
   950	.lp-btn .lp-ic {
   951	  transition: transform 0.16s var(--ease);
   952	}
   953	.lp-btn:hover .lp-ic {
   954	  transform: translateX(2px);
   955	}
   956	
   957	/* ----- HERO ----------------------------------------------------------------- */
   958	.lp-hero {
   959	  padding: clamp(48px, 8vw, 92px) 0 clamp(40px, 6vw, 72px);
   960	}
   961	.lp-hero-grid {
   962	  display: grid;
   963	  grid-template-columns: 1.05fr 0.95fr;
   964	  gap: clamp(32px, 5vw, 56px);
   965	  align-items: center;
   966	}
   967	@media (max-width: 920px) {
   968	  .lp-hero-grid {
   969	    grid-template-columns: 1fr;
   970	    align-items: start;
   971	  }
   972	}
   973	.lp-hero h1 {
   974	  font-size: clamp(33px, 5.4vw, 56px);
   975	  line-height: 1.04;
   976	  letter-spacing: -0.02em;
   977	  margin: 0 0 20px;
   978	  max-width: 18ch;
   979	}
   980	.lp-hero .lp-lede {
   981	  font-size: clamp(16px, 2vw, 19px);
   982	  max-width: 52ch;
   983	}
   984	.lp-hero-aside {
   985	  position: relative;
   986	}
   987	@media (max-width: 920px) {
   988	  .lp-hero-aside {
   989	    max-width: 520px;
   990	  }
   991	}
   992	
   993	/* ----- panels + grids (restrained cards: hairline, tinted hover, no glow) ---- */
   994	.lp-grid3 {
   995	  display: grid;
   996	  gap: 14px;
   997	  margin-top: 26px;
   998	}
   999	.lp-grid2 {
  1000	  display: grid;
  1001	  gap: 14px;
  1002	  margin-top: 24px;
  1003	}
  1004	@media (min-width: 720px) {
  1005	  .lp-grid3 {
  1006	    grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr);
  1007	  }
  1008	  .lp-grid2 {
  1009	    grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr;
  1010	  }
  1011	}
  1012	.lp-panel {
  1013	  border: 1px solid var(--rule);
  1014	  border-radius: 12px;
  1015	  padding: 22px;
  1016	  background: var(--bg);
  1017	  transition:
  1018	    border-color 0.16s ease,
  1019	    box-shadow 0.16s ease,
  1020	    transform 0.16s var(--ease);
  1021	}
  1022	.lp-panel:hover {
  1023	  border-color: var(--accent-line);
  1024	  box-shadow: 0 12px 30px -20px rgba(74, 14, 26, 0.2);
  1025	  transform: translateY(-2px);
  1026	}
  1027	.lp-panel.ours {
  1028	  border-color: var(--accent-line);
  1029	  background: var(--accent-soft);
  1030	}
  1031	.lp-role {
  1032	  font-family: var(--ff-mono);
  1033	  font-size: 11.5px;
  1034	  letter-spacing: 0.01em;
  1035	  color: var(--graphite);
  1036	  margin-bottom: 10px;
  1037	}
  1038	.lp-panel.ours .lp-role {
  1039	  color: var(--signal-text);
  1040	  font-weight: 600;
  1041	}
  1042	.lp-panel .lp-h3 {
  1043	  margin-bottom: 6px;
  1044	}
  1045	.lp-qh {
  1046	  font-family: var(--serif);
  1047	  font-weight: 600;
  1048	  font-size: 16px;
  1049	  line-height: 1.32;
  1050	  letter-spacing: -0.012em;
  1051	  color: var(--ink);
  1052	  margin-bottom: 6px;
  1053	}
  1054	
  1055	/* ----- HOW IT WORKS — five stages as a hairline-connected strip -------------- */
  1056	.lp-flow {
  1057	  margin-top: 28px;
  1058	  display: grid;
  1059	  grid-template-columns: repeat(5, 1fr);
  1060	  border: 1px solid var(--rule-2);
  1061	  border-radius: 12px;
  1062	  overflow: hidden;
  1063	}
  1064	.lp-step {
  1065	  padding: 20px 18px;
  1066	  border-left: 1px solid var(--rule);
  1067	  background: var(--bg);
  1068	  transition: background 0.16s ease;
  1069	}
  1070	.lp-step:first-child {
  1071	  border-left: none;
  1072	}
  1073	.lp-step.gate {
  1074	  background: var(--accent-soft);
  1075	}
  1076	.lp-sk {
  1077	  display: block;
  1078	  font-family: var(--ff-mono);
  1079	  font-size: 10px;
  1080	  letter-spacing: 0.07em;
  1081	  text-transform: uppercase;
  1082	  color: var(--muted);
  1083	  margin-bottom: 6px;
  1084	}
  1085	.lp-step.gate .lp-sk {
  1086	  color: var(--signal-text);
  1087	}
  1088	.lp-step .lp-h4 {
  1089	  margin-bottom: 6px;
  1090	}
  1091	@media (max-width: 760px) {
  1092	  .lp-flow {
  1093	    grid-template-columns: 1fr;
  1094	  }
  1095	  .lp-step {
  1096	    border-left: none;
  1097	    border-top: 1px solid var(--rule);
  1098	  }
  1099	  .lp-step:first-child {
  1100	    border-top: none;
  1101	  }
  1102	}
  1103	
  1104	/* ----- CLOSE — honesty note + mono strip ------------------------------------ */
  1105	.lp-note {
  1106	  margin: 26px 0 0;
  1107	  border: 1px solid var(--rule);
  1108	  background: var(--bg-3);
  1109	  border-radius: 10px;
  1110	  padding: 14px 16px;
  1111	  font-size: 13px;
  1112	  line-height: 1.6;
  1113	  color: var(--body);
  1114	  max-width: 82ch;
  1115	}
  1116	.lp-strip {
  1117	  margin: 16px 0 0;
  1118	  padding-top: 14px;
  1119	  border-top: 1px solid var(--rule);
  1120	  font-size: 11px;
  1121	  line-height: 1.8;
  1122	  letter-spacing: 0.005em;
  1123	  color: var(--faint);
  1124	  max-width: 82ch;
  1125	}
  1126	
  1127	@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
  1128	  .lp-btn,
  1129	  .lp-btn .lp-ic,
  1130	  .lp-panel,
  1131	  .lp-step {
  1132	    transition: none;
  1133	  }
  1134	}
  1135	
  1136	/* ============================================================================
  1137	   THE "LEDGER" DESIGN SYSTEM (owner-picked 2026-07-08) — a Swiss / International-
  1138	   Typographic audit certificate on a gallery-white ground. Scoped to the two real
  1139	   verifier surfaces (/report + /demo, both mount .rpt-wrap); the landing keeps its
  1140	   own system untouched. Reference (adapted, never copied):
  1141	   mockups/design-samples-2026-07-08/sample-1-ledger.html.
  1142	
  1143	   Ultramarine is the ONE accent — SIMULATED tag block · masthead rule · finding
  1144	   index numerals — NEVER on severity. Severity carries its own three marks
  1145	   (error / warn / info), kept apart from the accent. No radii, no shadows, no
  1146	   gradients: the precision of the hairline rules and the spacing IS the design.
  1147	   Desktop web + printable (see the @media print block below). rpt-* / dmo-*.
  1148	   ========================================================================== */
  1149	.rpt-wrap {
  1150	  /* Ledger tokens — pre-decided; #ffffff is the owner gallery-white modification
  1151	     (the sample used #f9f9f6). Kept as ONE --paper token so text-on-ink surfaces
  1152	     (the banner text, the FAIL flag) follow to #ffffff automatically. */
  1153	  --paper: #ffffff; /* ground + every text-on-ink surface (banner text, verdict flag) */
  1154	  --ink: #101014; /* near-black ink: headline, banner ground, verdict stamp, hard marks */
  1155	  --body: #33333b; /* long-form body text */
  1156	  --graphite: #50505a; /* rail captions, labels, secondary mono */
  1157	  --hairline: #dcdcd4; /* the modular-grid rules (1px, warm-on-white by intent) */
  1158	  --um: #1b3eb8; /* ultramarine — the ONE accent */
  1159	  --sev-err: #a8231b;
  1160	  --sev-warn: #8a5a06;
  1161	  --sev-info: #55555e;
  1162	  /* system font stacks only — no new dependency. Grotesk display + a tabular mono. */
  1163	  --ledger-sans: "Helvetica Neue", Helvetica, Arial, "Liberation Sans", sans-serif;
  1164	  --ledger-mono: ui-monospace, "SF Mono", SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Consolas, "Liberation Mono",
  1165	    monospace;
  1166	
  1167	  max-width: 1180px;
  1168	  margin: 0 auto;
  1169	  padding: 48px 64px 120px;
  1170	  background: var(--paper);
  1171	  color: var(--body);
  1172	  font-family: var(--ledger-sans);
  1173	  font-size: 15px;
  1174	  line-height: 1.55;
  1175	  letter-spacing: 0;
  1176	}
  1177	
  1178	/* ---- SIMULATED banner — solid ink band, unmissable, first thing on the page.
  1179	   Text uses --paper (#ffffff) on the ink ground; survives print exact. ---- */
  1180	.rpt-sim {
  1181	  display: flex;
  1182	  align-items: flex-start;
  1183	  gap: 22px;
  1184	  padding: 20px 24px;
  1185	  border: none;
  1186	  border-radius: 0;
  1187	  background: var(--ink);
  1188	  -webkit-print-color-adjust: exact;
  1189	  print-color-adjust: exact;
  1190	}
  1191	.rpt-sim-tag {
  1192	  flex: none;
  1193	  font-family: var(--ledger-mono);
  1194	  font-size: 12px;
  1195	  font-weight: 700;
  1196	  letter-spacing: 0.16em;
  1197	  color: var(--paper);
  1198	  background: var(--um);
  1199	  padding: 7px 12px;
  1200	  border-radius: 0;
  1201	  -webkit-print-color-adjust: exact;
  1202	  print-color-adjust: exact;
  1203	}
  1204	.rpt-sim-text {
  1205	  min-width: 0;
  1206	  margin: 2px 0 0;
  1207	  font-size: 13.5px;
  1208	  line-height: 1.6;
  1209	  color: var(--paper);
  1210	  max-width: 92ch;
  1211	}
  1212	
  1213	/* ---- masthead — flush-left, closed by the single 4px ultramarine rule ---- */
  1214	.rpt-head {
  1215	  margin-top: 0;
  1216	  padding: 44px 0 30px;
  1217	  border-bottom: 4px solid var(--um);
  1218	}
  1219	.rpt-eyebrow {
  1220	  margin: 0;
  1221	  font-family: var(--ledger-sans);
  1222	  font-size: 11px;
  1223	  font-weight: 700;
  1224	  letter-spacing: 0.18em;
  1225	  text-transform: uppercase;
  1226	  color: var(--graphite);
  1227	}
  1228	.rpt-title {
  1229	  margin: 16px 0 0;
  1230	  font-family: var(--ledger-sans);
  1231	  font-size: clamp(34px, 4.4vw, 52px);
  1232	  font-weight: 800;
  1233	  letter-spacing: -0.03em;
  1234	  line-height: 1.02;
  1235	  color: var(--ink);
  1236	  max-width: 24ch;
  1237	  text-wrap: balance;
  1238	}
  1239	.rpt-intro {
  1240	  margin: 22px 0 0;
  1241	  font-size: 15.5px;
  1242	  line-height: 1.6;
  1243	  color: var(--body);
  1244	  max-width: 68ch;
  1245	}
  1246	
  1247	/* ---- surface toggle — a flush mono tab strip on a hairline; ink underline for
  1248	   the active surface (ultramarine is reserved for its three sanctioned roles) -- */
  1249	.rpt-toolbar {
  1250	  display: flex;
  1251	  gap: 0;
  1252	  margin-top: 28px;
  1253	  border-bottom: 1px solid var(--hairline);
  1254	}
  1255	.rpt-tab {
  1256	  font: inherit;
  1257	  font-family: var(--ledger-mono);
  1258	  font-size: 12px;
  1259	  letter-spacing: 0.04em;
  1260	  cursor: pointer;
  1261	  padding: 10px 16px 11px;
  1262	  border: none;
  1263	  border-bottom: 2px solid transparent;
  1264	  border-radius: 0;
  1265	  background: none;
  1266	  color: var(--graphite);
  1267	  margin-bottom: -1px;
  1268	  transition:
  1269	    color 0.15s ease,
  1270	    border-color 0.15s ease;

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	"use client";
     2	
     3	import { useEffect, useRef, type ReactNode } from "react";
     4	
     5	/**
     6	 * A scroll-reveal wrapper. SSR-renders settled (no opacity-0 in the static HTML), so the page
     7	 * is fully readable with JS disabled and under reduced motion. When motion is allowed, it strips
     8	 * the settled state on mount, then re-adds `.in` as the block scrolls into view — a one-shot
     9	 * stagger that never hides content from the initial DOM.
    10	 */
    11	export function Reveal({
    12	  children,
    13	  stagger = false,
    14	  className = "",
    15	  as: Tag = "div",
    16	}: {
    17	  children: ReactNode;
    18	  stagger?: boolean;
    19	  className?: string;
    20	  as?: "div" | "section";
    21	}) {
    22	  const ref = useRef<HTMLElement | null>(null);
    23	
    24	  useEffect(() => {
    25	    const el = ref.current;
    26	    if (!el || typeof window === "undefined") return;
    27	    if (window.matchMedia("(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)").matches) return;
    28	
    29	    // motion allowed: opt this block into the animated lifecycle, then reveal on intersect
    30	    el.classList.add(stagger ? "ds-stagger" : "ds-reveal");
    31	    el.classList.remove("in");
    32	
    33	    const io = new IntersectionObserver(
    34	      (entries) => {
    35	        for (const e of entries) {
    36	          if (e.isIntersecting) {
    37	            e.target.classList.add("in");
    38	            io.unobserve(e.target);
    39	          }
    40	        }
    41	      },
    42	      { threshold: 0.12 },
    43	    );
    44	    io.observe(el);
    45	    return () => io.disconnect();
    46	  }, [stagger]);
    47	
    48	  const cls = [stagger ? "ds-stagger in" : "ds-reveal in", className].filter(Boolean).join(" ");
    49	
    50	  return Tag === "section" ? (
    51	    <section ref={ref as React.RefObject<HTMLElement>} className={cls}>
    52	      {children}
    53	    </section>
    54	  ) : (
    55	    <div ref={ref as React.RefObject<HTMLDivElement>} className={cls}>
    56	      {children}
    57	    </div>
    58	  );
    59	}

docs/decision-log.md:132:| 2026-07-08 | **WHOLE-SITE REDESIGN — DIRECTION + IDENTITY + STEPS FIXED (owner, eighteenth session cont.; AskUserQuestion structured answers, verbatim selections).** The whole-site redesign (all 10 routes + shell + content) was delivered mockup-first in `mockups/ultra-modern-2026-07-08/` (owner directives + verbatim quotes durably recorded in that dir's README/build-spec/story-arc: the `/claude-os` whole-site directive "ultramodern Premium UI, gallery white, redesign whole website all layout + content, without training knowledge/memory" → the v2 revision "remove the grid · keep gallery white · dark red/maroon/burgundy · UI+MOTION+ICONS+FONTS · story-arc narrative · simple in depth & breadth, domain-expert · fullest capabilities"). This turn the three writer-flagged open items were put to the owner and answered: **① Direction = "Oxblood v2 (burgundy)"** (`whole-site-gallery-v2.html` — premium off-white #FBFBFD ground, ONE burgundy accent #8A2233, grid removed, serif-display+mono, custom monoline SVG icons, scroll-motion, story-arc content); **② Identity = "Unify → Commerce Truth Audit"** (retire the page-title "ActivationOps" + console "Curbside Commons" from the site; the S-11 real-brand pick, e.g. Plumbline, stays a separate open owner act and would rename later); **③ TOTAL_STEPS = 5 confirmed** (landing pipeline copy correct as built). This FIRES the gated product-code implementation (rewrite `app/globals.css` + all pages/components + content, under the full per-slice gate). **Ground reconciliation flagged, not silently resolved:** v2's #FBFBFD vs the already-implemented `/report` `--paper #FFFFFF` (decision-log 2026-07-08 Ledger pick) — to be reconciled at implementation planning (the whole-site rewrite supersedes the interim Ledger `/report` styling, so #FBFBFD becomes the sitewide ground unless the owner says otherwise). Build routes to Opus lane, judged at the Fable-equivalence bar (design-quality standard, this date); deploy stays a SEPARATE owner act (design-first ruling 2026-07-03). | Accepted (owner, 2026-07-08) | Owner answered the three surfaced open items directly; the mockup + copy-deck + story-arc are the spec; unify-to-one-name removes the tri-name inconsistency; ground reconciliation surfaced for the plan | This row; `mockups/ultra-modern-2026-07-08/` (README, build-spec, copy-deck, story-arc, whole-site-gallery-v2.html); AskUserQuestion answers 2026-07-08 |

     8	
     9	// Fixed reference points (no wall-clock; owner may change the as-of date).
    10	export const AS_OF_DATE = "2026-06-01";
    11	export const RUN_TIMESTAMP = "2026-06-01T00:00:00Z";
    12	export const TASK_ID = "T-001"; // lineage tag carried from the Python prototype

mockups/ultra-modern-2026-07-08/whole-site-copy-deck.md:110:- **`<details>` opt-in (kept):** "Why a tone/safety filter structurally can't catch this." Body: faithfulness is a relation between text and a record, not a property of the text; only reconciling each asserted fact against the structured source of truth catches it. *(`page.tsx:218–234`.)*
mockups/ultra-modern-2026-07-08/whole-site-copy-deck.md:114:- **Headline (kept):** "One made-up claim, held — checked against the merchant's own record." *(`page.tsx:244–246`.)*

codex
The mockup/spec comparison supports the builder escalations so far: S2 follows the 7-section mockup landing, uses bare monoline icons instead of the mockup’s `.icon-badge` tiles per the plan ban, caps landing radii at 12px, and changes the 5-step flow into a connected strip rather than separated cards.

     1	# Semantic Judge — P3 Calibration Status
     2	
     3	**Status (2026-06-25): the clean calibration run RAN ($0, cross-family Groq `gpt-oss-120b`). The HELD-OUT (test) split CLEARED the pre-registered bar.** The result is **DIRECTIONAL** (held-out n=15, ~30-item gold set) — the "built + calibrated, metrics = X" claim still waits for **P4** (eval-lock + Codex gate) and the **~100+ validation floor** (R-HON-1/3).
     4	
     5	> **A3-3 note (R-A3-8, 2026-06-28) — the directional label is NOT upgraded by the multi-agent loop.** A3-3 swapped the loop's Drafter to **Gemini Flash** (cross-family — Gemini drafts, this Groq judge checks). The metrics above were calibrated over the **synthetic gold set**, NOT over **live Gemini prose** (a new draft distribution). Running this judge inside the A3 loop adds **no** evidence about its faithfulness calibration; the label stays **"directional, pending the ~100 floor."** Re-calibration / K re-pinning on the live Gemini distribution is **owner-gated at A3-7** (R-A3-9).
     6	
     7	## The run (2026-06-25)
     8	
     9	- Live cross-family judge: Groq `openai/gpt-oss-120b`, strict structured outputs, `reasoningEffort:"low"`, temp 0, **K=3 reps/item over the 30-item gold set**. **$0** (free tier). Snapshot: `lib/data/judge-calibration.snapshot.json`.
    10	- **Pre-registered pass/fail bar (pinned BEFORE reading numbers, on the held-out test split — R-CAL-7):** recall **≥ 0.78**, precision **≥ 0.70** (reported), Cohen's κ **≥ 0.60** (raw agreement ≥ 0.80), test-retest flip-rate **≤ 0.10**.
    11	
    12	## Metrics (gatekeeper-passing subset, R-CAL-1)
    13	
    14	| Split | n | Recall | Precision | F1 | Matrix (tp/fp/tn/fn) |
    15	|---|---|---|---|---|---|
    16	| **Held-out (test) — THE bar** | 15 | **1.000** (CI95 0.70–1.0) | **1.000** | 1.000 | 9 / 0 / 6 / 0 |
    17	| Tune | 13 | 0.714 | 0.833 | 0.769 | 5 / 1 / 5 / 2 |
    18	| Overall | 28 | 0.875 | 0.933 | 0.903 | 14 / 1 / 11 / 2 |
    19	
    20	- Cohen's κ (judge vs gold, passing subset): **0.784** (substantial; raw agreement 0.893).
    21	- Test-retest flip-rate (K=3): **0.071**.
    22	
    23	**Verdict: the held-out split CLEARS all four pre-registered thresholds** — recall 1.0 ≥ 0.78; precision 1.0 ≥ 0.70; κ 0.78 ≥ 0.60 (agreement 0.89 ≥ 0.80); flip 0.071 ≤ 0.10. The held-out split was committed in the gold set before the run (not chosen after), so this is honest pre-registration, not goalpost-moving.
    24	
    25	## Honest caveats (NOT buried)
    26	
    27	1. **The live runner reported FAIL on an over-strict assertion — fixed (test-harness bug, NOT a judge change).** Line 168 asserted *every* gatekeeper-passing item carries ≥1 claim, but **clean drafts legitimately return 0 unsupported claims** (`C-mock-2/3`, `C-mock-grocery`, …) and 2 flippy tune-split positives returned a clean rep-0. Corrected to the real plumbing invariant: a draft the judge **flags** must carry its claim breakdown (clean drafts may have 0); quality thresholds stay eval-locked at P4 on the FROZEN report (R-HON-2/3). **Verified against the recorded snapshot — NOT re-run** (the shared 200K/day Groq window is not re-burned).
    28	2. **The tune split is weaker** (recall 0.714, precision 0.833): 2 false-negatives the judge flips on at temp 0 (`P-timeline-3` "wraps up early next week"-class; `P-specific-3`) and 1 debatable false-positive (`C-live-sunset`'s "listed on Curbside Commons" — the platform-grounding edge). **Real, bounded failure modes — do not overclaim a "perfect judge."**
    29	3. **Small n** (held-out 15; gold 30) → **DIRECTIONAL** (R-HON-1). The ~100+ floor is the eventual validation target; **no "calibrated, metrics = X" public claim before P4 + the Codex gate** (R-HON-3).
    30	
    31	## Effect on the build
    32	
    33	- **AM-1 satisfied:** P3 completed and cleared the held-out bar → **A2's LIVE self-correction milestone (R-LOOP-10) is UNBLOCKED.** R-LOOP-10 operates on exactly the 9 held-out planted positives, **all of which the judge caught** (recall 1.0 there) — a strong catcher for the loop.
    34	- **Remaining judge work (P4 — deferred, owner/Codex-gated):** eval-lock the bar against the FROZEN snapshot (offline regression, R-HON-2 — assert the fixture, never a live re-run); the Codex cross-model gate on the calibration honesty; only then flip the docs from "designed boundary" → "built + calibrated, metrics = X"; grow toward the ~100+ floor. The 3 SHOWABLE surfaces (R-DEMO-2/3/4) render the recorded fixture at $0.
    35	
    36	## How to re-run (P4 / fresh window only — do NOT re-burn the window casually)
    37	
    38	```
    39	node --env-file=.env node_modules/.bin/vitest run evals/judge-calibration.live.test.ts
    40	```
    41	~30K of the 200K/day shared Groq window; auto-skips offline (`npm test` does not load `.env`).

     1	# Domain Judge — B1 Calibration Status
     2	
     3	**Status (2026-06-26): the LIVE calibration RAN ($0, cross-family Groq `openai/gpt-oss-120b`), the HELD-OUT (test) split CLEARED all seven pre-registered thresholds, AND the mandatory Codex cross-model gate has now RUN** (gpt-5.5 @ xhigh over the full B1 diff → **2 P2 code findings, 0 P0/P1, both fixed + reconciled primary-model-final + test-locked** — `docs/reviews/codex-2026-06-26-b1-domain-judge.md`). Per **R-DHON-3** (metrics exist + clear the pre-registered bar + the Codex gate cleared) the **METHODOLOGY is now CALIBRATED**; per **R-DHON-1** the **METRIC stays DIRECTIONAL** — the gold set is *synthetic* and the held-out n=18 sits below the ~100 validation floor. So the honest label is **"calibrated — directional, pending the ~100 validation floor"**, NEVER a bare "calibrated." The pass/fail **bar was PRE-REGISTERED below BEFORE any live number was read** (R-DCAL-7), and the held-out split was committed in the gold set before the run — so clearing it is honest pre-registration, not goalpost-moving. The result is **eval-locked** (`evals/domain-calibration.lock.test.ts` asserts the frozen `lib/data/domain-calibration.snapshot.json` clears the bar; it makes NO live call — R-DHON-4). **Scope note (do not over-read the gate):** `codex review` audits *code*, not prose claims — it discharged the gate as "ran + no blocking findings"; the word "calibrated" is carried by the bar-clearance + eval-lock + the independent **acceptance-gate** + the documented **R-DARCH-2 leak-check**, not by Codex's silence on the docs.
     4	
     5	> **A3-3 note (R-A3-8, 2026-06-28) — the "calibrated — directional" label is NOT upgraded by the multi-agent loop.** A3-3 swapped the loop's **Drafter** to **Gemini Flash** (cross-family). This domain judge becomes the loop's **Domain Critic** only at **A3-4** (it is NOT wired into the loop in A3-3); and its calibration above was over the **synthetic gold set**, NOT over **live Gemini prose** (a new draft distribution). Neither the A3-3 drafter swap nor the future A3-4 wiring adds evidence about its calibration; the label stays **"calibrated — directional, pending the ~100 floor."** Re-calibration on the live Gemini distribution is **owner-gated at A3-7** (R-A3-9).
     6	
     7	This is the **Effective**-axis analogue of the faithfulness judge's `docs/judge-calibration-status.md`.
     8	
     9	## What is built (offline, $0, committed)
    10	
    11	- **Rubric** (`lib/domain/effective-rubric.ts`) — the 3 dimensions under calibration (matched-to-blocker · engagement-appropriate · no-over-promise) as a KB-cited STANDARD; `domainSituation()` surfaces the situation WITHOUT leaking the answer (R-DARCH-2). `docs/spec-domain-judge.md` = the spec.
    12	- **Judge** (`lib/agents/domain-judge.ts`) — per-dimension Zod verdict; deterministic mock (stub baseline, $0); live cross-family Groq `gpt-oss-120b` via DI, budget-guarded + fail-closed.
    13	- **Gold set** (`evals/gold/domain-gold.ts`) — 24 single-dimension synthetic positives (8 per dimension) + 12 clean negatives; every positive is a matched draft with only its body swapped, so it is gate-passing + faithful by construction (the labels are objective).
    14	- **Harness + offline test** (`evals/gold/domain-harness.ts`, `evals/domain-calibration.test.ts`) — reuses `lib/evals/judge-metrics.ts`; **R-DCAL-1 enforced LIVE** (every item run through the real `runGatekeeper` + the faithfulness mock — this already caught + fixed one mis-constructed gold item where "once you're live" tripped `state_mismatch`); per-dimension reporting; the mock is run ONLY as a labeled stub baseline, never gated.
    15	- **Live runner** (`evals/domain-calibration.live.test.ts`) — key-gated, auto-skips offline.
    16	
    17	## The PRE-REGISTERED bar (pinned BEFORE the run, on the held-out **test** split — R-DCAL-7)
    18	
    19	Recall-favoring (a false flag just sends a fine draft to a human; a missed bad-practice draft is the costly miss). Reported on the **territory** (gate-passing + faithful) subset (R-DCAL-1).
    20	
    21	| Metric | Bar | Note |
    22	| --- | --- | --- |
    23	| **Aggregate held-out recall** | **≥ 0.80** | ≥ 10/12 domain-defective test drafts caught |
    24	| **Aggregate held-out precision** | **≥ 0.70** | reported at that operating point |
    25	| **Per-dimension recall — matched_to_blocker** | **≥ 0.75** | ≥ 3/4 (the clean, `diagnose()`-anchored core) |
    26	| **Per-dimension recall — engagement_appropriate** | **≥ 0.75** | ≥ 3/4 (the clean, `diagnose()`-anchored core) |
    27	| **Per-dimension recall — no_over_promise (§4.2)** | **≥ 0.50** | ≥ 2/4; the fuzziest dimension, softer floor (see caveat 2) |
    28	| **Cohen's κ (judge vs gold, territory subset)** | **≥ 0.60** | substantial agreement (Landis–Koch) |
    29	| **Test-retest flip-rate (K=3, temp 0)** | **≤ 0.15** | a flippy judge corrupts the regression lock |
    30	
    31	**Decision rule.** IF the held-out split clears ALL of the above → eval-lock (freeze `lib/data/domain-calibration.snapshot.json`; add an offline regression test asserting the FROZEN fixture, never a live re-run — R-DHON-4) + flip the docs from "designed rubric" → "built + calibrated, metrics = X" (R-DHON-3) + the Codex gate. ELSE → tune the prompt/threshold on the **tune** split + re-run; **never tune on the test split.**
    32	
    33	## The run + results (2026-06-26) — held-out CLEARED all seven (directional)
    34	
    35	Live cross-family judge: Groq `openai/gpt-oss-120b`, strict structured outputs, `reasoningEffort:"low"`, temp 0, **K=3 reps/item over the 36-item gold set**. **$0** (free tier). **36/36 real `LIVE_JUDGE` verdicts, 0 fallbacks.** Snapshot: `lib/data/domain-calibration.snapshot.json` (frozen; eval-locked by `evals/domain-calibration.lock.test.ts`).
    36	
    37	**Aggregate (territory subset, R-DCAL-1):**
    38	
    39	| Split | n | Recall | Precision | F1 | Matrix (tp/fp/tn/fn) |
    40	|---|---|---|---|---|---|
    41	| **Held-out (test) — THE bar** | 18 | **1.000** (CI95 0.76–1.0) | **1.000** | 1.000 | 12 / 0 / 6 / 0 |
    42	| Tune | 18 | 1.000 | 1.000 | 1.000 | 12 / 0 / 6 / 0 |
    43	| Overall | 36 | 1.000 | 1.000 | 1.000 | 24 / 0 / 12 / 0 |
    44	
    45	- Cohen's κ (judge vs gold, territory subset): **1.000**. Test-retest flip-rate (K=3): **0.000**.
    46	
    47	**Per-dimension held-out (test) recall (R-DCAL-2):**
    48	
    49	| Dimension | Recall | Precision | Note |
    50	|---|---|---|---|
    51	| matched_to_blocker | **1.000** (4/4) | 1.000 | clean |
    52	| engagement_appropriate | **1.000** (4/4) | **0.500** | precision dragged by cross-dim bleed — caveat 7 |
    53	| no_over_promise (§4.2) | **1.000** (4/4) | 1.000 | clean |
    54	
    55	**Verdict: the held-out split CLEARS all seven pre-registered thresholds** — recall 1.0 ≥ 0.80; precision 1.0 ≥ 0.70; per-dim recall 1.0 ≥ {0.75, 0.75, 0.50}; κ 1.0 ≥ 0.60; flip 0.0 ≤ 0.15.
    56	
    57	**No-answer-leakage verified (R-DARCH-2 — the make-or-break property).** The perfect-but-stable κ=1.0 / flip=0.0 signature was scrutinised (it is also what a tautology/wrapper would show): `domainSituation()` withholds `diagnose().play` / `.root_cause_hypothesis` (the answer); the live runner never passes the gold `dimension`/`failureMode`/label into the prompt; and the recorded rationales reason situation→draft *cold* (quoting the offending phrase) and **isolate the correct dimension** — e.g. on an engagement-defective ghosted draft the judge PASSES matched + over-promise and FAILS only engagement, which a label-fed wrapper could not do selectively. The engagement cross-dim bleed (caveat 7) is itself the fingerprint of independent reasoning, not leakage.
    58	
    59	## Honest caveats (NOT buried)
    60	
    61	1. **All gold positives are SYNTHETIC/planted** (R-DCAL-4): the recorded live Flash drafts are matched + well-grounded, so organic domain-defects ≈ 0. Metrics are measured on synthetic domain-defects; docs say so. Directional until the ~100 validation floor (R-DHON-1).
    62	2. **The over-promise dimension (§4.2) has only PARTIAL marginal value vs the LIVE faithfulness judge.** Its positives dodge the deterministic guardrail (verified) and the faithfulness *mock*, but an implied-typicality benefit ("stores like yours become favorites") can be borderline-unsupported, so the live faithfulness judge may also flag some. That is why §4.2 is isolated + reported per-dimension with a softer floor; **dimensions 1 (matched) + 2 (engagement) are the cleanly-marginal core** (a generic-but-truthful draft, or a bare nudge to a ghosted merchant, is invisible to BOTH the gate and faithfulness — only the domain judge catches it).
    63	3. **Platform-side escalation is DEFERRED, not faked** (R-DCAL-3): `lib/domain/diagnosis.ts` emits only `merchant_side`; calibrating platform-side would require fabricating data the synthetic model does not carry. Documented rubric rule; revisit at B2 with a real `blocker_source` signal.
    64	4. **Researched, not credentialed** (R-DHON-2 / AM-7): the rubric is researched + source-cited (the B0 KB) + owner judgment, never a marketplace-insider's expertise.
    65	5. **Small held-out N** (4 test positives/dimension): per-dimension recall is granular (0/.25/.5/.75/1.0). The ~100 floor is the later validation; B1's numbers are directional.
    66	6. **Difficulty / realism gap — where the directional number is WEAKEST.** The positives are hand-authored to be *cleanly* defective ("Complete your setup!"); a real Gemini drafter rarely emits something that obvious, so live recall on these synthetic positives may **overstate** recall on subtle real-world defects. And in production most drafts are *good*, so the judge's **precision on realistic clean drafts is the production-critical number** — yet the gold set has only **2 real-supply clean negatives** (the rest are deterministic mock-clean). Growing real-supply negatives + subtler positives is a priority for the ~100 floor.
    67	7. **engagement_appropriate per-dim PRECISION = 0.5 (a measured result, not a flaw hidden).** The judge flags the generic `matched_to_blocker` (D1) drafts as ALSO engagement-inappropriate (a generic draft fits no engagement state well — a reasoned, debatable stance, NOT answer-leakage). This drags engagement per-dim precision to 0.5 but does NOT dent aggregate precision (those drafts are true positives at the draft level). The pre-registered bar carries no per-dim precision floor; the question of whether `engagement_appropriate` should fire on generic drafts is carried explicitly to the **B2 §4.2 / dimension-redundancy decision** (Forward-decision section below). The eval-lock asserts this precision is `< 1` so the nuance stays visible, never silently blessed.
    68	
    69	## How it was run (2026-06-26) — re-run command (fresh Groq daily window only)
    70	
    71	It was run once cleanly on 2026-06-26 (a one-call smoke first, to protect the daily budget against a strict-output/fallback failure, then the full ~100K-token run). To reproduce on a fresh window:
    72	
    73	```
    74	# 1) put a free GROQ_API_KEY in the gitignored .env (editor, never chat)
    75	# 2) re-verify the model is current (RULES §6): gpt-oss-120b non-deprecated + Groq strict-output on
    76	# 3) run (≈36 items × 3 reps, ~14s pacing ≈ 25–30 min, ~100K tokens of the 200K/day budget, $0):
    77	node --env-file=.env node_modules/.bin/vitest run evals/domain-calibration.live.test.ts
    78	# 4) read lib/data/domain-calibration.snapshot.json → compare held-out + per-dimension to the bar above
    79	```
    80	
    81	Do not run a heavy Groq job on another project concurrently (the 200K/day window is shared).
    82	
    83	## Remaining B1 work after the run
    84	
    85	The bar cleared AND the Codex gate ran, so B1 is **DONE**:
    86	- **DONE — eval-lock:** `evals/domain-calibration.lock.test.ts` (committed `1fcb492`); `verify` green (now **250 + 4 skipped** after the Codex-fix lock tests); `acceptance-gate` engineering = SHIP (leak / non-vacuity / eval-lock / metric-math all cleared independently); the doc-coherence BLOCK is closed.
    87	- **DONE — Codex cross-model gate:** RAN on a fresh seat (gpt-5.5 @ xhigh, `~/claude-os/bin/codex-guarded review --base 07e9a55`, full B1 diff) → **2 P2 code findings, 0 P0/P1, both fixed + reconciled primary-model-final + test-locked** (`docs/reviews/codex-2026-06-26-b1-domain-judge.md`). Discharges R-DHON-3's gate condition AND the B1-offline obligation (one base covers B1a→B1d).
    88	- **DONE — docs flipped (this update):** "RAN + CLEARED (directional)" → **"calibrated — directional, pending the ~100 floor"** (methodology calibrated; metric directional) — coherently across `PROJECT_STATE` / `CURRENT_TASK` / `HANDOFF` / the eval-lock header / the prior gate records.
    89	- **NEXT — B2:** wire the KB into the agents + the domain judge into the ship gate; settle the §4.2 dimension-redundancy decision below. Then **A3** (4 bounded agents + Gemini Flash drafter ≤ $5 + cross-family judge).
    90	- The **~100 validation floor (R-DHON-1)** keeps the number **directional** regardless — it is the ONLY thing now between this and an unqualified "calibrated."
    91	
    92	## Forward decision for B2 — DECIDED (owner, 2026-06-26)
    93	
    94	**§4.2 ordering / redundancy — DECISION: keep `no_over_promise` as a gating domain dimension (detection) AND feed KB §4.2 into the A3 Drafter prompt (prevention) = defense in depth.** At runtime the faithfulness judge runs BEFORE the domain judge (R-DARCH-4: gatekeeper → faithfulness → domain). The overlap is only **partial**, not redundant: faithfulness checks per-claim entailment against the merchant's **own data row**, so it structurally **cannot** flag an implied-typicality / general-hype claim ("stores like yours quickly become favorites") — that is not a claim about this merchant's data, so there is no field to entail it against — yet it is a clear over-promise (KB §4.2 rule 1). The dimension is recall-favoring + never auto-rejects, so the marginal cost of keeping it is ~nil (a redundant flag just routes to the human who already reviews). **Owner-approved 2026-06-26 (AskUserQuestion); decision-log 2026-06-26.** The drafter-prompt half lands in **A3** (when the Drafter exists); a **Codex cross-check on the wiring** is due at B2 implementation (consequential-recommendation rule). `matched_to_blocker` + `engagement_appropriate` were never in question — invisible to both the gate and faithfulness, so load-bearing regardless.

docs/decisions/ADR-002-multi-agent-architecture.md:11:ActivationOps AI is a **portfolio/capability showcase** (goal-locked 2026-06-22), not a venture or an operated service. The near-ship product — a deterministic core (differential-locked to a Python oracle) + a bounded Gemini drafter + a claims-gatekeeper + a cross-family Groq semantic judge + an eval harness + hybrid data + a REPLAY snapshot — is, by Anthropic's taxonomy, a **workflow, not an agent** (two sequential LLM calls inside deterministic plumbing). The owner asked to elevate it into a genuine, recommendation-grade **multi-agent** AI product that solves merchant activation end-to-end, on a free/free-tier industry-standard stack, with researched domain expertise grounding *and* evaluating the content — quality over tokens/speed.
docs/decisions/ADR-002-multi-agent-architecture.md:23:- **Two-axis ship bar:** ship only if **Faithful** (every claim verified vs the data row) **AND Effective** (domain judge ≥ calibrated threshold, Track B).
docs/decisions/ADR-002-multi-agent-architecture.md:31:3. **A2's same-family verify (Groq drafts, Groq judges) proves control-flow convergence only** — calibrated faithfulness requires A3 cross-family (AM-3).
docs/decisions/ADR-002-multi-agent-architecture.md:62:- **The paused P3 judge calibration MUST complete and clear the held-out bar** (recall/precision/F1 + κ + flip-rate, R-CAL-7) and be eval-locked — the "self-correcting" payoff is **contingent** on a calibrated detector existing (AM-1; `docs/judge-calibration-status.md`). Until then A2 = offline loop-machinery only (R-LOOP-8).
docs/decisions/ADR-002-multi-agent-architecture.md:66:Before A1 build starts: Phase 0 cleared (this ADR + the 3 decision-log reversals + state sync + owner `/autopilot` GO). No "built + calibrated / multi-agent / self-correcting" public claim ships before its evidence exists and a Codex gate approves (R-HON-3).
evals/gold/fee-baseline-measurement.test.ts:17: * nothing here is a "calibrated" claim about any model. The headline split is TEST
evals/gold/fee-baseline-measurement.test.ts:39:  it("baseline classifier never claims to be earned/calibrated", () => {
docs/task-log.md:75:**Did:** Oriented on the A3 spec + the A2 single-agent loop it splits (`lib/agents/loop/orchestrator.ts`, `tools/registry.ts`, `loop/trajectory.ts`). Called the **advisor** (stronger-model cross-check) BEFORE committing to the architecture → it validated the boundary/contract/§4.2-wiring and sharpened: apply AM-2 to the Strategist too (must beat `diagnose().play`); the Router is the anti-theater crux (must beat `buildReflection`); don't manufacture a 4th agent to hit "4"; both judges stay Groq (Drafter→Gemini restores cross-family R-ARCH-3); add per-`agent` trajectory attribution now; HOLD the "directional" label (judges calibrated on the gold set, not yet on live Gemini prose); gate each agent separately, offline-first; re-set K. Surfaced the agent-count as an **owner decision** (AskUserQuestion) → owner chose **"Target the full 4"** (build all four, each gated by a seam-proof).
docs/task-log.md:101:## 2026-06-26 (Track B1 — Codex cross-model gate RAN + reconciled → domain judge "calibrated — directional"; 2 P2 fixes)
docs/task-log.md:109:**Flip (R-DHON-3 + R-DHON-1):** docs flipped coherently in ONE pass — "directional / pending Codex" → **"calibrated — directional, pending the ~100 floor"** across `docs/domain-calibration-status.md` (line-3 logic rewritten, not find/replaced), `evals/domain-calibration.lock.test.ts` (header), PROJECT_STATE / CURRENT_TASK / HANDOFF, + forward-pointers on the two prior gate records. The "calibrated" word is carried by the cleared bar + eval-lock + acceptance-gate + the R-DARCH-2 leak-check, NOT by Codex's silence (it audits code, not prose).
docs/task-log.md:113:**Honesty / owner gate:** committed 2026-06-26 (owner GO via "continue"); introducing "calibrated" into the honesty-sensitive docs is a public-claim change — owner-approved. **Push remains owner-gated** (RULES §12); not pushed. Skills/tools: advisor (×3 this session), codex-guarded (the gate), acceptance-gate (prior, referenced). NEXT = B2 + A3.
docs/task-log.md:127:**Honesty held (R-DHON-1/3):** "RAN + CLEARED (DIRECTIONAL)", NOT "calibrated" — the calibrated label waits for the Codex gate + the ~100 floor; all gold positives SYNTHETIC (R-DCAL-4). Skills/tools: advisor (×3), acceptance-gate, codex-guarded (attempted). Commit per green slice (owner mode). No push (owner-gated).
docs/task-log.md:137:**Gates:** `acceptance-gate` = **SHIP** (offline machinery, framed as method-not-calibrated-judge). Codex changed-files review = **SEAT-BLOCKED** (usage limit, raw-surfaced, ~3:27 PM reset) → dated obligation folded into the B1d Codex gate. Durable record: `docs/reviews/gate-2026-06-26-b1-offline.md`.
docs/task-log.md:139:**Honesty held:** NO "calibrated" claim anywhere; the live Groq calibration run + eval-lock + Codex gate are owner-gated (B1d, R-DHON-3). State docs synced to "B1 offline machinery done", never "B1 done". Skills used: advisor (review), acceptance-gate. Commit per green slice (owner mode). No push (owner-gated).
docs/task-log.md:170:## 2026-06-22 (semantic judge — P3 infrastructure + live judge wired + calibration run; owner provided GROQ_API_KEY)

docs/task-log.md:186:One clean calibration run on a fresh Groq daily window → held-out metrics → P4 (eval-lock + demo surfaces + Codex gate + flip docs ONLY if metrics clear the bar, R-HON-3).
